content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

May 16, 2006

Michael L. Farley
Farley Law Firm
1120 West Main Street
Visalia, CA 93291

RE: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-06-081

Dear Mr. Farley:

This letter is in response to your request on behalf of the City of Farmersville regarding the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹

Specifically, you wished to know whether the city has any reporting obligations under California Health and Safety Code Section 33130 with respect to Councilmember Paul Boyer's 1031 tax exchange involving property located on Oak View Avenue.

Please note that the Commission is not permitted to offer advice on bodies of law outside the confines of the Act. Therefore, we cannot offer written advice with regard to the above inquiry. In addition, the Commission does not provide advice relating to past conduct and can only advise prospectively. (Regulation 18329(b)(8)(A), enclosed.)

If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: Emelyn Rodriguez
Counsel, Legal Division

Enclosure
ER:rd
I:\AdviceLtrs\06-081

¹ Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections 18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.

In addition, the Commission does not provide advice relating to past conduct and can only advise prospectively.
sections.question_synthetic: Does the City of Farmersville have reporting obligations under California Health and Safety Code Section 33130 regarding a councilmember's 1031 tax exchange transaction?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declined to provide written advice because the inquiry involves California Health and Safety Code provisions outside the Political Reform Act's scope, and the Commission does not advise on past conduct.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Declination letter citing two bases: (1) subject matter outside Political Reform Act scope, and (2) inquiry relates to past conduct rather than prospective guidance. No traditional Q/A sections but clear question and conclusion present.

CONCLUSION: The Commission is not permitted to offer advice on bodies of law outside the confines of the Act. Therefore, we cannot offer written advice with regard to the above inquiry. In addition, the Commission does not provide advice relating to past conduct and can only advise prospectively.

embedding.summary: The FPPC declined to provide advice regarding whether the City of Farmersville has reporting obligations under California Health and Safety Code Section 33130 concerning a councilmember's 1031 tax exchange, citing jurisdictional limitations and its policy against advising on past conduct.
